prs.title: Added number guessing game
prs.body:
This PR adds a fun and interactive number-guessing game to the repository. The game challenges users to guess a random number between 1 and 50 with an intelligent hint system.

✨ Features Added

- Random Number Generation: Generates a random number between 1-50.

- Smart Hint System:

       > -  Higher/Lower directional hints
       > -  Even/Odd number hints
       > -   Temperature-based proximity hints (hot/warm/cold) after 3 attempts

- Attempt Tracking: Displays the number of attempts made

- Reset Functionality: Allows users to start a new game anytime

- Responsive Design: Works seamlessly across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices

- Modern UI: Beautiful gradient design with smooth animations

📁 Files Added/Modified

1. index.html - Game structure and layout
2. style.css - Styling with gradients, animations, and responsive design
3. script.js - Game logic, hint system, and event handlers
4. README.md - Comprehensive documentation
5. image.png - Demo of the project

prs.title: Issue 51: Add 10 Beginner DSA Problems in Python with Solutions
prs.body:
This PR adds a set of 10 beginner-level DSA problems implemented in Python, each in its own file under interview_preparation/python_dsa_beginner/.

Each problem includes a docstring explaining the problem, constraints, and time/space complexity.

A tests/ folder includes pytest unit tests for each problem.

A README.md lists all problems, their difficulty, and example inputs/outputs for easy reference.

Problems included:
  1. Reverse a string
  2. Factorial (iterative)
  3. Check prime
  4. Fibonacci (nth)
  5. Two-sum (simple)
  6. Merge two sorted lists (array)
  7. Remove duplicates from list
  8. Valid parentheses
  9. Count vowels
  10. Binary search
  
 Testing: All tests can be run using:
              pytest tests/

prs.title: added orgs
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- New Features
  - Import repositories from your GitHub account when managing organization repositories.
  - Create organizations from the profile via a new Add button.
  - View organization repositories with basic issue details (title, status, bounty amount) after authentication.

- Improvements
  - Enhanced authorization checks before fetching organization data, with clearer error messages for unauthorized or missing access.
  - AddRepo now accepts repositories provided by the page, enabling server-fetched data to drive the UI.
  - Minor UI tweaks in the repository list (e.g., simplified item display).

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: added org
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- New Features
  - Introduced an Org section to import repositories with a guided flow.
  - Added a repository browser with search, multi-select language filters, colored language chips, and detailed cards (stars/tokens).
  - Implemented per-repository confirmation dialogs before import.
  - Added navigation to individual repository pages from the list.

- Navigation
  - Updated sidebar links for Profile and Leaderboard to new routes.

- Pages
  - Added new pages for Org landing and Add Repos, rendering the new repository import and browsing experience.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- New Features
  - Repositories view with search and language filters.
  - Issues view with search and tag filters, including empty-state messaging.
  - Repository-specific issues page with back navigation.
  - Tabbed navigation to switch between Repositories and Issues.
  - Logout now shows a confirmation dialog.
  - Persistent sidebar layout with automatic active-state highlighting.

- Chores
  - Added UI and auth dependencies to support dialogs, selects, and tabs.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- New Features
  - App-wide theming with dark/system preferences applied globally.
  - Collapsible, mobile-friendly sidebar with keyboard toggle (Ctrl/Cmd + B).
  - New Repositories page scaffold and sidebar-powered main layout.
  - Sheet panels and tooltips for richer interactions.

- Improvements

- Chores

prs.title: My Web Dev Story
prs.body:
# 📝 Pull Request Template

Thank you for contributing to **Open Source Learning Stories**! 🎉  
Please make sure your PR follows the guidelines below.

---

## ✅ Checklist

- ✅ I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md).  
- ✅ My story is added inside the `stories/` folder.  
- ✅ The file is named correctly: `your-name.md`.  
- ✅ I used the proper story format (Name, Date, Tools, Experience, Advice).  
- ✅ I did not edit or delete someone else’s story.  
- ✅ My contribution is genuine and respectful.  

---

## 📖 Description

Please provide a short description of your contribution:  

(e.g., “Added my open-source journey story,” “Fixed typo in example-story.md,” etc.)

---

## 📂 Related Issue (if any)

Closes # (issue number)

---

## 🚀 Screenshots / Preview (Optional)

(Add a screenshot if relevant, e.g., before/after changes in docs or design)

---

💡 *Tip:* Keep your PR small and focused on one change. It makes reviewing easier. 🙌
